blockingIssues: Step 3 says `WhatIfPanel` should use `useAiStatus()` to hide the panel when AI is unavailable. That conflicts with Step 2 and the codebase’s graceful-degradation model: the what-if computation is deterministic backend logic, and only the narrative is AI-dependent. The backend plan explicitly says to return structured impact analysis with `narrative = null` when AI is unavailable. Hiding the panel on provider unavailability would make the feature inaccessible precisely when it should still work. The plan should instead keep the panel available whenever the feature flag/page state allows it, and degrade only the narrative/recommendation portion when AI is down.
